devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं स भगवान् वैन्यः ख्यापितो गुणकर्मभिः । छन्दयामास तान् कामैः प्रतिपूज्याभिनन्द्य च ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaevaṃ sa bhagavān vainyaḥ khyāpito guṇakarmabhiḥchandayāmāsa tān kāmaiḥ pratipūjyābhinandya ca
translation:
Maitreya said:
King Pṛthu, the illustrious son of Vena, who was thus eulogised for his excellent attributes and exploits, satisfied those bards by honouring them with gifts as desired by them and by appreciating them.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणप्रमुखान् वर्णान् भृत्यामात्यपुरोधसः । पौरान् जानपदान् श्रेणीः प्रकृतीः समपूजयत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇapramukhān varṇān bhṛtyāmātyapurodhasaḥpaurān jānapadān śreṇīḥ prakṛtīḥ samapūjayat
translation: He honoured all the classes of people of whom the Brāhmaṇas were foremost, and also his servants, ministers and family priests, the citizens as well as the rural public, various trade guilds and other prominent subjects.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । कस्माद्दधार गोरूपं धरित्री बहुरूपिणी । यां दुदोह पृथुस्तत्र को वत्सो दोहनं च किम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvācakasmāddadhāra gorūpaṃ dharitrī bahurūpiṇīyāṃ dudoha pṛthustatra ko vatso dohanaṃ ca kim
translation:
Vidura asked:
Why did the goddess of the earth who can assume multifarious forms assume the form of a cow? When Pṛthu milched her who became the calf and what was the milk-pail?

devanagari: प्रकृत्या विषमा देवी कृता तेन समा कथम् । तस्य मेध्यं हयं देवः कस्य हेतोरपाहरत् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: prakṛtyā viṣamā devī kṛtā tena samā kathamtasya medhyaṃ hayaṃ devaḥ kasya hetorapāharat
translation: How is it that he levelled the goddess of the earth who was by nature uneven? For what purpose did the god Indra cany off his sacrificial horse?

devanagari: सनत्कुमाराद्‍भगवतो ब्रह्मन् ब्रह्मविदुत्तमात् । लब्ध्वा ज्ञानं सविज्ञानं राजर्षिः कां गतिं गतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sanatkumārād‍bhagavato brahman brahmaviduttamātlabdhvā jñānaṃ savijñānaṃ rājarṣiḥ kāṃ gatiṃ gataḥ
translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa (Maitreya)! To what state did the royal-sage attain after getting initiated in the knowledge of the Brahman and its personal experience[1] from the venerable Sanatkumāra, the best among the knowers of Brahman?
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Jñānaṃ savijñānam—In B.G. III.41, Śaṅkara explains jñāna as ‘knowledge of the self and other things acquired from the scriptures and the teachers’ and vijñāna as ‘the personal experience—anubhava of things so taught’.

devanagari: यच्चान्यदपि कृष्णस्य भवान् भगवतः प्रभोः । श्रवः सुश्रवसः पुण्यं पूर्वदेहकथाश्रयम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yaccānyadapi kṛṣṇasya bhavān bhagavataḥ prabhoḥśravaḥ suśravasaḥ puṇyaṃ pūrvadehakathāśrayam
translation: I am a votary devoted to you as well as to Lord Kṛṣṇa (Adhokṣaja). Your honour should narrate to me many other deeds of Lord Kṛṣṇa of sanctifying glory, connected with the account of his former manifestation as Pṛthu. Kṛṣṇa in the form of Pṛthu has milched this cow in the form of the earth.

devanagari: भक्ताय मेऽनुरक्ताय तव चाधोक्षजस्य च । वक्तुमर्हसि योऽदुह्यद् वैन्यरूपेण गामिमाम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktāya me'nuraktāya tava cādhokṣajasya cavaktumarhasi yo'duhyad vainyarūpeṇa gāmimām
translation: I am a votary devoted to you as well as to Lord Kṛṣṇa (Adhokṣaja). Your honour should narrate to me many other deeds of Lord Kṛṣṇa of sanctifying glory, connected with the account of his former manifestation as Pṛthu. Kṛṣṇa in the form of Pṛthu has milched this cow in the form of the earth.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । चोदितो विदुरेणैवं वासुदेवकथां प्रति । प्रशस्य तं प्रीतमना मैत्रेयः प्रत्यभाषत ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvācacodito vidureṇaivaṃ vāsudevakathāṃ pratipraśasya taṃ prītamanā maitreyaḥ pratyabhāṣata
translation:
Sūta said:
When he was thus urged by Vidura to narrate the story of Vāsudeva, Maitreya, who was pleased with him in mind, praised him and replied.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । यदाभिषिक्तः पृथुरङ्ग विप्रैः । आमंत्रितो जनतायाश्च पालः । प्रजा निरन्ने क्षितिपृष्ठ एत्य । क्षुत्क्षामदेहाः पतिमभ्यवोचन् ...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācayadābhiṣiktaḥ pṛthuraṅga vipraiḥāmaṃtrito janatāyāśca pālaḥprajā niranne kṣitipṛṣṭha etyakṣutkṣāmadehāḥ patimabhyavocan
translation:
Maitreya said:
Dear child (Vidura)! When Pṛthu was coronated and was called as the protector of the public, the subjects with their bodies emaciated with hunger due to barrenness of the earth, approached Pṛthu, the protector and appealed.

devanagari: वयं राजञ्जाठरेणाभितप्ता । यथाग्निना कोटरस्थेन वृक्षाः । त्वामद्य याताः शरणं शरण्यं । यः साधितो वृत्तिकरः पतिर्नः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ rājañjāṭhareṇābhitaptāyathāgninā koṭarasthena vṛkṣāḥtvāmadya yātāḥ śaraṇaṃ śaraṇyaṃyaḥ sādhito vṛttikaraḥ patirnaḥ
translation: “Oh King! We are oppressed by the gastric fire just like the wood with fire in its hollow. Today we have approached you for shelter, as you are installed as our king who will provide us with means of livelihood, and are worthy of seeking asylum.

devanagari: तन्नो भवानीहतु रातवेऽन्नं । क्षुधार्दितानां नरदेवदेव । यावन्न नङ्क्ष्यामह उज्झितोर्जा । वार्तापतिस्त्वं किल लोकपालः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tanno bhavānīhatu rātave'nnaṃkṣudhārditānāṃ naradevadevayāvanna naṅkṣyāmaha ujjhitorjāvārtāpatistvaṃ kila lokapālaḥ
translation: Oh King of kings! You are verily the protector of the people and the bestower of the means of livelihood. Therefore, while we have not perished from starvation, Your Majesty should endeavour to supply food to us who are famished with hunger.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । पृथुः प्रजानां करुणं निशम्य परिदेवितम् । दीर्घं दध्यौ कुरुश्रेष्ठ निमित्तं सोऽन्वपद्यत ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācapṛthuḥ prajānāṃ karuṇaṃ niśamya paridevitamdīrghaṃ dadhyau kuruśreṣṭha nimittaṃ so'nvapadyata
translation:
Maitreya said:
Oh great Kuru! Having listened to the heart-rending lamentations of his subjects, Pṛthu contemplated deeply for a long time, and at last came to perceive the cause of the scarcity.

devanagari: इति व्यवसितो बुद्ध्या प्रगृहीतशरासनः । सन्दधे विशिखं भूमेः क्रुद्धस्त्रिपुरहा यथा ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vyavasito buddhyā pragṛhītaśarāsanaḥsandadhe viśikhaṃ bhūmeḥ kruddhastripurahā yathā
translation: By his reasoning power he came to the conclusion (that the earth had held up the seeds of herbs, plants and food-grains within herself). Being indignant, he took up his bow and fitted an arrow on it aiming at the earth, just as the enraged destroyer of Tripura (Śiva, did against Tripura).

devanagari: प्रवेपमाना धरणी निशाम्योदायुधं च तम् । गौः सत्यपाद्रवद्‍भीता मृगीव मृगयुद्रुता ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: pravepamānā dharaṇī niśāmyodāyudhaṃ ca tamgauḥ satyapādravad‍bhītā mṛgīva mṛgayudrutā
translation: When the earth saw the king with his weapon uplifted, she began to tremble. She assumed the form of a cow and in terror began to run fast like a terrified female deer chased by a hunter.

devanagari: तामन्वधावत्तद्वैन्यः कुपितोऽत्यरुणेक्षणः । शरं धनुषि सन्धाय यत्र यत्र पलायते ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmanvadhāvattadvainyaḥ kupito'tyaruṇekṣaṇaḥśaraṃ dhanuṣi sandhāya yatra yatra palāyate
translation: The enraged son of Vena, with eyes blood-red with wrath, and with the arrow fitted to his bow, chased after her whithersoever she ran (for shelter).

devanagari: सा दिशो विदिशो देवी रोदसी चान्तरं तयोः । धावन्ती तत्र तत्रैनं ददर्शानूद्यतायुधम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sā diśo vidiśo devī rodasī cāntaraṃ tayoḥdhāvantī tatra tatrainaṃ dadarśānūdyatāyudham
translation: Running to all the directions (cardinal points) and intermediate directions (e.g. South-east, South-west etc.), to the heaven and the earth and in the space between them, the goddess Earth saw him chasing her everywhere with uplifted weapon.

devanagari: लोके नाविन्दत त्राणं वैन्यान्मृत्योरिव प्रजाः । त्रस्ता तदा निववृते हृदयेन विदूयता ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: loke nāvindata trāṇaṃ vainyānmṛtyoriva prajāḥtrastā tadā nivavṛte hṛdayena vidūyatā
translation: Just as people do not get protection from death, she did not get any asylum in the world against Pṛthu, the son of Vena. Terrified, she returned with a troubled heart.

devanagari: उवाच च महाभागं धर्मज्ञापन्नवत्सल । त्राहि मामपि भूतानां पालनेऽवस्थितो भवान् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāca ca mahābhāgaṃ dharmajñāpannavatsalatrāhi māmapi bhūtānāṃ pālane'vasthito bhavān
translation: And she spoke to the illustrious king, “Oh Knower of Dharma! you are kind to the afflicted. Determined as your majesty is on the protection of creatures, protect me as well.

devanagari: स त्वं जिघांससे कस्माद् दीनामकृतकिल्बिषाम् । अहनिष्यत्कथं योषां धर्मज्ञ इति यो मतः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ jighāṃsase kasmād dīnāmakṛtakilbiṣāmahaniṣyatkathaṃ yoṣāṃ dharmajña iti yo mataḥ
translation: Such as you are, why do you intend to kill me who am afflicted and innocent? How could a person who is regarded as expert in (the niceties of) Dharma, strike women?

devanagari: प्रहरन्ति न वै स्त्रीषु कृतागःस्वपि जन्तवः । किमुत त्वद्विधा राजन् करुणा दीनवत्सलाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: praharanti na vai strīṣu kṛtāgaḥsvapi jantavaḥkimuta tvadvidhā rājan karuṇā dīnavatsalāḥ
translation: Oh King! Beings (people) do not at all strike the females even though they have committed an offence. Much less would persons like you who are merciful and compassionate to the helpless, do so, Oh King.

devanagari: मां विपाट्याजरां नावं यत्र विश्वं प्रतिष्ठितम् । आत्मानं च प्रजाश्चेमाः कथं अम्भसि धास्यसि ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: māṃ vipāṭyājarāṃ nāvaṃ yatra viśvaṃ pratiṣṭhitamātmānaṃ ca prajāścemāḥ kathaṃ ambhasi dhāsyasi
translation: How can you support yourself and these subjects of yours on the surface of (cosmic) waters after destroying me—a strong boat wherein the whole universe is established?”

devanagari: पृथुरुवाच । वसुधे त्वां वधिष्यामि मच्छासनपराङ्मुखीम् । भागं बर्हिषि या वृङ्क्ते न तनोति च नो वसु ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthuruvācavasudhe tvāṃ vadhiṣyāmi macchāsanaparāṅmukhīmbhāgaṃ barhiṣi yā vṛṅkte na tanoti ca no vasu
translation:
Pṛthu said:
“Oh Earth! I want to kill you as you disobey my commands—You partake of your share in the sacrifices, but you do not yield us wealth.

devanagari: यवसं जग्ध्यनुदिनं नैव दोग्ध्यौधसं पयः । तस्यामेवं हि दुष्टायां दण्डो नात्र न शस्यते ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yavasaṃ jagdhyanudinaṃ naiva dogdhyaudhasaṃ payaḥtasyāmevaṃ hi duṣṭāyāṃ daṇḍo nātra na śasyate
translation: It is not that punishment is disapproved in the case of a wicked cow which every day eats fodder but does not yield milk from her udders.

devanagari: त्वं खल्वोषधिबीजानि प्राक्सृष्टानि स्वयम्भुवा । न मुञ्चस्यात्मरुद्धानि मामवज्ञाय मन्दधीः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ khalvoṣadhibījāni prāksṛṣṭāni svayambhuvāna muñcasyātmaruddhāni māmavajñāya mandadhīḥ
translation: You are dull-witted. You have confined in yourself the seeds of plants etc. created already by god Brahmā. You do not allow them to grow, thus you set me at nought.

devanagari: अमूषां क्षुत्परीतानां आर्तानां परिदेवितम् । शमयिष्यामि मद्‍बाणैः भिन्नायास्तव मेदसा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: amūṣāṃ kṣutparītānāṃ ārtānāṃ paridevitamśamayiṣyāmi mad‍bāṇaiḥ bhinnāyāstava medasā
translation: I shall pacify the afflictions of these subjects who are famished with hunger and are distressed, with your flesh cut down by my arrows.

devanagari: पुमान्योषिदुत क्लीब आत्मसम्भावनोऽधमः । भूतेषु निरनुक्रोशो नृपाणां तद्वधोऽवधः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: pumānyoṣiduta klība ātmasambhāvano'dhamaḥbhūteṣu niranukrośo nṛpāṇāṃ tadvadho'vadhaḥ
translation: To kill a vile self-conceited fellow—whether a man, a woman or an impotent person—who is merciless to others, is no killing at all, in the case of kings.

devanagari: त्वां स्तब्धां दुर्मदां नीत्वा मायागां तिलशः शरैः । आत्मयोगबलेनेमा धारयिष्याम्यहं प्रजाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tvāṃ stabdhāṃ durmadāṃ nītvā māyāgāṃ tilaśaḥ śaraiḥātmayogabalenemā dhārayiṣyāmyahaṃ prajāḥ
translation: You are obstinate, arrogant and have assumed a false form of a cow. Having shattered you to sesamum seed like pieces with my arrows, I shall support these subjects by the dint of my Yogic power.”

devanagari: एवं मन्युमयीं मूर्तिं कृतान्तमिव बिभ्रतम् । प्रणता प्राञ्जलिः प्राह मही सञ्जातवेपथुः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ manyumayīṃ mūrtiṃ kṛtāntamiva bibhratampraṇatā prāñjaliḥ prāha mahī sañjātavepathuḥ
translation: The earth began to tremble. She bowed to him who like the god of death[2] bore a form which was wrath incarnate, and spoke to him with folded hands.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains that the body of the god of death is not made of anger.

devanagari: धरोवाच । नमः परस्मै पुरुषाय मायया । विन्यस्तनानातनवे गुणात्मने । नमः स्वरूपानुभवेन निर्धुत । द्रव्यक्रियाकारकविभ्रमोर्मये ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: dharovācanamaḥ parasmai puruṣāya māyayāvinyastanānātanave guṇātmanenamaḥ svarūpānubhavena nirdhutadravyakriyākārakavibhramormaye
translation:
The Earth-goddess said:
“I pay my obeisance again and again to the Supreme Man who manifested his various forms by his Māyā (power), and appears to be composed of the (three) guṇas, though (in reality) by the realization of his essential nature; he has been ever free from the waves of love, hate (etc.) born of ahaṃkāra (his false identification) with regard to matter (adhibhūta), action (adhyātma) and the agent (adhidaiva).

devanagari: येनाहमात्मायतनं विनिर्मिता । धात्रा यतोऽयं गुणसर्गसङ्ग्रहः । स एव मां हन्तुमुदायुधः स्वराड् । उपस्थितोऽन्यं शरणं कमाश्रये ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yenāhamātmāyatanaṃ vinirmitādhātrā yato'yaṃ guṇasargasaṅgrahaḥsa eva māṃ hantumudāyudhaḥ svarāḍupasthito'nyaṃ śaraṇaṃ kamāśraye
translation: That Supreme Being, the very Creator who created me as the abode of all jīvas (living beings) and as the support of the four classes of creatures composed of the guṇas of Prakṛti, has come, with uplifted arm, to strike me down! To whom else can I resort now for shelter?

devanagari: य एतदादावसृजच्चराचरं । स्वमाययात्माश्रययावितर्क्यया । तयैव सोऽयं किल गोप्तुमुद्यतः । कथं नु मां धर्मपरो जिघांसति ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etadādāvasṛjaccarācaraṃsvamāyayātmāśrayayāvitarkyayātayaiva so'yaṃ kila goptumudyataḥkathaṃ nu māṃ dharmaparo jighāṃsati
translation: He who, at the beginning of creation, created this world of mobiles and immobiles by his supra-logical Māyā Power which rests on him (for support) is said to be now ready to protect it by dint of the same Power. Devoted to righteousness (and its promotion) as he is, how does he desire to kill me?

devanagari: नूनं बतेशस्य समीहितं जनैः । तन्मायया दुर्जययाकृतात्मभिः । न लक्ष्यते यस्त्वकरोदकारयद् । योऽनेक एकः परतश्च ईश्वरः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ bateśasya samīhitaṃ janaiḥtanmāyayā durjayayākṛtātmabhiḥna lakṣyate yastvakarodakārayadyo'neka ekaḥ parataśca īśvaraḥ
translation: The Almighty, though one, appears as many on account of the other, i.e. the Māyā Power. He created god Brahmā and made him evolve the universe. Alas, the intention of the Lord cannot at all be fathomed by people whose minds are distracted by the invincible Māyā.

devanagari: सर्गादि योऽस्यानुरुणद्धि शक्तिभिः । द्रव्यक्रियाकारक चेतनात्मभिः । तस्मै समुन्नद्धनिरुद्धशक्तये । नमः परस्मै पुरुषाय वेधसे ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sargādi yo'syānuruṇaddhi śaktibhiḥdravyakriyākāraka cetanātmabhiḥtasmai samunnaddhaniruddhaśaktayenamaḥ parasmai puruṣāya vedhase
translation: I bow to the Highest Person, the Creator (of the world) who manifests and withdraws his powers (suitable for the occasion or purpose), and who brings about the creation (preservation and destruction) of the universe by means of his potencies in the form of substance (i.e. five gross elements—mahābhūtas), action (senses and deities presiding over senseorgans), the principle of intelligence (buddhi) and ego (ahaṃkāra).

devanagari: स वै भवानात्मविनिर्मितं जगद् । भूतेन्द्रियान्तःकरणात्मकं विभो । संस्थापयिष्यन्नज मां रसातलाद् । अभ्युज्जहाराम्भस आदिसूकरः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai bhavānātmavinirmitaṃ jagadbhūtendriyāntaḥkaraṇātmakaṃ vibhosaṃsthāpayiṣyannaja māṃ rasātalādabhyujjahārāmbhasa ādisūkaraḥ
translation: Oh birthless Almighty! It is Your Majesty who as the primeval Boar verily lifted me up from the waters of Rasātala, with the intention of establishing the world created by you and consisting of five gross-elements, sense-organs and the inner organ (the heart).

devanagari: अपामुपस्थे मयि नाव्यवस्थिताः । प्रजा भवानद्य रिरक्षिषुः किल । स वीरमूर्तिः समभूद् धराधरो । यो मां पयस्युग्रशरो जिघांससि ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: apāmupasthe mayi nāvyavasthitāḥprajā bhavānadya rirakṣiṣuḥ kilasa vīramūrtiḥ samabhūd dharādharoyo māṃ payasyugraśaro jighāṃsasi
translation: Today that very Boar (the upholder of the earth) has appeared as the hero (Pṛthu) who desires to protect his subjects who have resorted to me as on board a ship on the surface of the waters. Your Majesty whose arrows are terrible, certainly desires to kill me for the sake of milk.

devanagari: नूनं जनैरीहितमीश्वराणां । अस्मद्विधैस्तद्‍गुणसर्गमायया । न ज्ञायते मोहितचित्तवर्त्मभिः । तेभ्यो नमो वीरयशस्करेभ्यः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ janairīhitamīśvarāṇāṃasmadvidhaistad‍guṇasargamāyayāna jñāyate mohitacittavartmabhiḥtebhyo namo vīrayaśaskarebhyaḥ
translation:
Oh Warrior! The intention of sovereign rulers (like you) is certainly not comprehended by people like us, whose paths of minds are deluded by the Māyā which creates the universe consisting of three guṇas. I pay my respects to those kings who bring glories to all.”
[back to top]
